item.title: Zimbabwe Opens US$1.5 Million Lithium Transshipment Rail Siding To Boost Mineral Exports
item.description: Zimbabwe Opens US$1.5 Million Lithium Rail Siding To Boost Mineral Exports Zimbabwe has strengthened its mineral export infrastructure after commissioning a US$1.5 million lithium transshipment rail siding in West Nicholson, Matabeleland South. The new facility has already handled its first shipment of lithium concentrate from Gwanda, with the consignment transported by rail to the Port [...] The post Zimbabwe Opens US$1.5 Million Lithium Transshipment Rail Siding To Boost Mineral Exports appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Double Data Bonus: Good News for Econet Customers as the Mobile Network Operator launches XtraData Bundles @econetzimbabwe
item.description: HARARE – In an era where digital connectivity has transitioned from a luxury to an absolute necessity, Zimbabwe’s largest mobile network operator, Econet Wireless, has made a decisive move to consolidate its market leadership. The company has officially pulled the curtain back on its new “XtraData Bundles,” a strategic offering that provides customers with up [...] The post Double Data Bonus: Good News for Econet Customers as the Mobile Network Operator launches XtraData Bundles first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Top schools head to Old Mutual National High Schools Quiz Finals
item.description: Online Reporter The battle for supremacy in the Old Mutual National High Schools Quiz is heating up after provincial champions emerged from across Zimbabwe, setting the stage for a fiercely contested national final on 24 July, 2024. Schools from ten provinces showcased their academic prowess in a series of highly competitive contests, with some champions cruising to victory while others had to fight until the final round to secure their place among the country’s elite. Midlands produced the highest-scoring performance of the provincial rounds, with Fletcher High School delivering a commanding display to finish on 78 points, comfortably ahead of Anderson High School on 54 and Merywood High School on 42. The emphatic victory sends a strong warning to rivals ahead of the national showdown. In Bulawayo, Northlea High School stamped its authority on the competition, collecting 62 points to finish ahead of St Columbus High School (36) and Maranatha Adventist High School (34). Northlea’s consistency throughout the contest proved decisive as it secured provincial honours. One of the closest contests unfolded in Mashonaland East, where Goromonzi High School edged Waddilove High School by just two points in a thrilling finish. Goromonzi emerged victorious with 68 points, while Waddilove finished on 66. Kwenda High School was not far behind on 60 points, highlighting the depth of competition in the province. Manicaland champions Nyanga High Marist Brothers matched Goromonzi’s winning score of 68 points to secure top spot in their province. Vhengere High School put up a strong challenge with 60 points, while St Faith’s High School completed the podium places with 48 points. In Matabeleland South, St Mary’s High School of Beitbridge produced a solid performance to claim the provincial title with 56 points. Mtsabezi High School followed on 46 points, while Fountain High School finished third with 44 points. Mashonaland Central saw St Albert’s High School emerge victorious with 54 points. Langham Girls High School and Mazowe Boys High School could not be separated after both finished on 36 points, sharing second place in one of the more unusual outcomes of the provincial campaign. Marist Brothers Dete successfully defended Matabeleland North’s academic pride, topping the standings with 46 points ahead of Gebhuza Secondary School on 34 and Hwange High School on 30. Meanwhile, in Masvingo Province, St Anthony’s High School secured first place with 50 points after fending off strong competition from Pamushana High School, which finished on 46 points. Zimuto High School claimed third place on 38 points. With provincial bragging rights secured, attention now turns to the national finals, where the champions will face off against the best schools from across the country in pursuit of the coveted national title. Based on the provincial performances, Fletcher High School, Goromonzi High School, Nyanga High Marist Brothers and Northlea High School are among the early contenders, but as the provincial rounds have shown, every point matters and no result can be taken for granted. Old Mutual Zimbabwe Marketing, Public Affairs and Sustainability Executive, Lillian Mbayiwa, said the quiz reflects the organisation’s broader commitment to education and youth development. “Education remains a key pillar of our responsible business strategy. Through initiatives like the Old Mutual National High Schools Quiz, we aim to provide platforms for students to demonstrate their academic prowess while also strengthening their leadership skills. As we prepare to welcome the 30 participating schools for the 43rd edition of the Old Mutual National High Schools Quiz, the stage is set for an exciting celebration of knowledge, talent, and collaboration that will inspire learners and highlight the importance of academic excellence,” she said. Mbayiwa added that the initiative goes beyond competition, serving as a platform for broader youth development programmes. “Old Mutual has also expanded its engagement to include initiatives that address societal challenges and broaden students’ perspectives, through programmes such as financial literacy talks, drug and substance abuse awareness in partnership with Cimas, as well as mentorship and career guidance for students. The goal is to build positive futures by ensuring that young people are not only academically strong, but also well-rounded and prepared for life beyond school,” she said. The countdown to The Old Mutual National High Schools Quiz Finals has begun set for 24 July, 2026, and Zimbabwe’s brightest young minds are preparing for the ultimate academic showdown. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Chewore Lodge Owners Find Peace After 15-Year Battle As They Relocate To Illala
item.description: Chewore Lodge Owners Confirm Move After Government-Brokered Settlement Ends 15-Year Dispute A months-long dispute over Chewore Lodge has come to an end after the lodge’s owners confirmed they had reached a negotiated settlement with the Government and will relocate their operations. The announcement comes just months after the family warned they were preparing legal action [...] The post Chewore Lodge Owners Find Peace After 15-Year Battle As They Relocate To Illala appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwe Banks To Be Linked To Deeds Office In Crackdown On Fake Property Titles
item.description: Zimbabwe’s Deeds Office To Give Banks Access In Crackdown On Fake Property Titles The Government is moving to strengthen the country’s property registration system by digitally connecting financial institutions to the Deeds Office, a move aimed at reducing fraud and making it easier to verify ownership of immovable property. Justice, Legal and Parliamentary Affairs Minister [...] The post Zimbabwe Banks To Be Linked To Deeds Office In Crackdown On Fake Property Titles appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: ZANU PF Harare Makes Surprise New Push To Keep Mnangagwa Until 2037 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: ZANU PF Harare Province Pushes To Extend Mnangagwa’s Presidency To 2037 Just days after President Emmerson Mnangagwa signed the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026, into law, the ruling party’s Harare Province has made a startling new demand. They are now pushing to extend his tenure beyond 2030, all the way to 2037. [...] The post ZANU PF Harare Makes Surprise New Push To Keep Mnangagwa Until 2037 appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: He Killed The Children First: Chilling New Details Of Eastview, Mabvuku Horror Emerge
item.description: The Chilling Sequence Police Believe Led To Mabvuku Family Tragedy A Harare family has been torn apart after a 24-year-old man allegedly killed his two young children before attacking their mother and setting their home on fire in Eastview, Mabvuku. Police say the children’s mother escaped despite suffering stab wounds, while the suspect is now [...] The post He Killed The Children First: Chilling New Details Of Eastview, Mabvuku Horror Emerge appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Chinhoyi Thief Returns Stolen TV, Begs for Mercy as Lots of Bees Torment Him After Family Consulted a Powerful Sangoma (VIDEO)
item.description: Mystical Justice: When Bees Deliver the Verdict In a world increasingly reliant on forensic science and conventional policing, a recent incident in Shackleton near Chinhoyi in Zimbabwe, has reignited discussions about traditional justice systems and the enigmatic power of what many refer to as ‘juju’. A video, widely circulated on social media, purportedly shows a [...] The post Chinhoyi Thief Returns Stolen TV, Begs for Mercy as Lots of Bees Torment Him After Family Consulted a Powerful Sangoma (VIDEO) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Premium Slot Gacor Games to Explore on Any Device
item.description: Looking for premium slot gacor games that run smoothly on your phone, tablet, or desktop without losing the features that matter? The best options are usually easy to load, simple to read on small screens, and built with clear controls that make every spin feel consistent instead of cramped or confusing. Device-friendly slot gacor play [...] The post Premium Slot Gacor Games to Explore on Any Device first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Over 1,400 passengers already booked seats on Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare-London direct flight – govt says
item.description: SOME 1,479 passengers have already booked their seats on state-owned airline Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare – London direct flight, Transport minister Felix Mhona has revealed. Mhona, who was speaking after receiving the 302-seater Airbus A330-300 aircraft at the Robert Gabriel Mugabe International Airport, also revealed that 30 tonnes of cargo had already been booked and [...] The post Over 1,400 passengers already booked seats on Air Zimbabwe’s new Harare-London direct flight – govt says appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Pensioners drag NSSA to court over ‘peanuts’ payouts
item.description: A group of pensioners has taken the National Social Security Authority (NSSA) to the High Court, accusing the pension body of violating their constitutional rights by paying what they describe as “grossly inadequate” pensions. The applicants, led by Gideon Shoko, are seeking a court order compelling NSSA to urgently review pension payouts to levels they [...] The post Pensioners drag NSSA to court over ‘peanuts’ payouts appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe processes 140,000 fleeing South Africa xenophobia
item.description: APA More than 140,000 nationals from Zimbabwe and Malawi have been repatriated from South Africa through the Beitbridge Border Post since early July, a senior official said on Monday. Chief Director in the Department of Immigration, Respect Gono, told the state-run media that an inter-ministerial committee is on the ground to support a safe and [...] The post Zimbabwe processes 140,000 fleeing South Africa xenophobia appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: 2030 no longer enough – ZANU PF now pushes to extend Mnangagwa’s presidency to 2037 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Barely days after President Emmerson Mnangagwa signed the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Act, 2026 into law, ZANU PF Harare Province has begun pushing to extend his tenure beyond 2030 to 2037. The latest call comes as the ruling party celebrates the enactment of the controversial constitutional amendment, which postpones Zimbabwe’s next harmonised elections [...] The post 2030 no longer enough – ZANU PF now pushes to extend Mnangagwa’s presidency to 2037 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Unfinished Transition: Why Stability Alone Cannot Deliver Prosperity
item.description: Economic recoveries often succeed twice. The first success is restoring stability. The second, if it comes, is restoring prosperity. The two are related, but they are not the same achievement. Inflation falls, markets begin functioning again, shops fill with goods and consumers regain confidence. After years of instability, these are the changes people naturally celebrate [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Unfinished Transition: Why Stability Alone Cannot Deliver Prosperity appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Harare man sets house on fire, killing two minor children
item.description: A 24-year-old Harare man is under police investigation for murder after allegedly setting fire to a room where his two young children were sleeping following a domestic dispute with their mother. The victims, Aldine Muyengwa (5) and Ropafadzo Muyengwa (2), died in the blaze at Eastview Phase 10, Mabvuku, at around 2:30am on Sunday, according [...] The post Harare man sets house on fire, killing two minor children appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News
